bead.title: agent: introduce attempt-cycle coordinator interfaces
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The current execute-bead implementation conflates worktree lifetime, implementation dispatch, artifact writing, landing, and post-merge review across ExecuteBeadWithConfig and the execute loop. Candidate-cycle review/repair needs a coordinator that owns the full live-worktree lifecycle before final land.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:668-680 documents ExecuteBeadWithConfig as the thin worker and says merge/update-ref/gate/preserve are parent responsibilities.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:765-767 removes the worktree via defer inside ExecuteBeadWithConfig, preventing same-worktree review/repair after the function returns.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:888-900 calls agenttry.Attempt around the executor, then handles review later in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1086-1104.
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:146-299 owns generic attempt outcome classification, but not candidate-cycle review/repair.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce narrow interfaces for AttemptCycleCoordinator, ImplementationPass, CandidateRefStore, CandidateCheckRunner, CandidateReviewer, RepairPass, and CandidateLander or equivalent names.
- Split the implementation pass from terminal worktree cleanup: implementation produces a candidate result inside a live worktree; the coordinator owns cleanup after terminal disposition.
- Preserve existing no_changes, no_evidence, and execution_failed behavior while creating seams for candidate checks, review, repair, and final land.
- Add basic cleanup liveness protection for active candidate-cycle worktrees before the deferred removal is moved.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not yet run pre-land review.
- Do not yet implement repair classification or repair loops.
- Do not enable two-slot review.

DEPS
Depends on the spec bead for vocabulary and on the read-only reviewer profile only for interface shape; this bead may define the coordinator before reviewer execution is moved.
bead.acceptance:
1. AttemptCycleCoordinator and the pass/check/review/repair/land interfaces (or equivalent names documented in code comments) exist in cli/internal/agent.
2. ExecuteBeadWithConfig or an extracted ImplementationPass can return a live worktree candidate result without owning terminal cleanup.
3. TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_SinglePassSuccessStillLands verifies the coordinator can preserve current success behavior without review/repair enabled.
4. TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_NoChangesBehaviorUnchanged verifies no_changes adjudication remains unchanged.
5. TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_LiveWorktreeProtected verifies active cycle metadata prevents cleanup from deleting the worktree mid-cycle.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestAttemptCycleCoordinator" green.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

events.body: {"rationale":"All 8 rubric criteria satisfied. (1) Title is imperative, names subsystem (agent) and change (coordinator interfaces). (2) Root cause cites four specific file:line ranges across execute_bead.go, execute_bead_loop.go, and try/attempt.go. (3) AC is numbered 1-8; items 3-5 name three distinct Test* functions; item 6 adds a -run filter. (4) Named tests in AC 3-5 are wired assertions against the specific behaviors introduced. (5) Final two AC lines are `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green` and `lefthook run pre-commit passes` — exact required gate. (6) Labels carry phase:2, area:agent, kind:refactor plus cross-refs adr:024 and spec:FEAT-010; extra tags (reliability, candidate-cycle) are benign. (7) Parent ddx-31e07fdc and deps ddx-71073116, ddx-e32a0f68 are explicit. (8) Description is fully self-contained — no /tmp paths, no chat references, no dangling 'see above'; dep bead references resolve to listed deps. One minor issue: AC item 1 hedges with 'or equivalent names documented in code comments', which softens verification determinism and gives the executor an escape hatch that could produce inconsistent interface names across attempts.","score":8,"suggested_fixes":["AC item 1: drop 'or equivalent names documented in code comments' and commit to the specific interface names listed in PROPOSED FIX (AttemptCycleCoordinator, ImplementationPass, CandidateRefStore, CandidateCheckRunner, CandidateReviewer, RepairPass, CandidateLander). Deterministic names make the acceptance check unambiguous."],"waivers_applied":[]}
